reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I’m certain my own Q1 answer is correct, and all recent puzzles have been simple arithmetic with a very high overall accuracy rate for Q2–Q4. In the last two games, someone (B or C) has volunteered immediately in Round 1, so the chance that at least one of B/C/D volunteers this round is high.

If I volunteer, my only way to die is if any of Q2–Q4 is wrong. Even a modest 10–15% chance that some other capsule is wrong is a 10–15% death risk for me. If I pass instead, I only risk random death if nobody volunteers: that’s 25% × P(no one volunteers), which is likely well under 10% given recent behavior. Passing also preserves all 4 of my coins, whereas verifying would cost a coin without reducing this round’s random-death risk.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: In all recent games, every verification has matched the existing capsules and, except for Game 1, the initial four-answer set has always formed a correct password. When I didn’t volunteer in Round 1 (Games 2 and 3), nobody else did either and we immediately faced a random elimination, giving me a 1-in-4 death chance regardless of how careful I’d been. Volunteering now swaps that random-death risk for the (slightly smaller) chance that one of the other three simple arithmetic answers is wrong, and lets me keep all 4 coins if the password is correct. With only single, non-conflicting capsules available for each question, the natural choice is to use Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q3 (96 - 58) is correct. Every game so far has used simple arithmetic, and aside from one early mistake by D, everyone’s answers have been consistently correct—even on first attempts with only single-owner capsules. Right now there are no conflicting capsules and no particular question looks suspicious.  

If I don’t volunteer, I still face the risk of random elimination this round if others also decide to wait, and spending a coin to verify something doesn’t reduce that immediate risk—it just leaves me poorer in later rounds. Given the strong track record and the simplicity of these puzzles, the chance that all four current capsules are correct is high enough that volunteering with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my odds of both surviving and keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ C entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
